no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly states 'Return the details value directly without applying a fallback when the value is an empty string' and 'Include a comment explaining the lenient fallback logic for action-like events that fail the strict type guard' — these are specific implementation directives telling the agent exactly what code changes to make, not just describing what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_get_event_content_source_v1 asserts the exact comment string 'Lenient fallback for action-like events' must appear in the source, and asserts 'details,' must appear. test_get_event_content_source_features also asserts 'details,' must be present. These are structural tests that enforce gold-specific implementation details — an alternative correct fix using a different comment wording or different variable pattern would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_get_event_content_source_v1 and test_get_event_content_source_features are purely text-matching tests (assert 'details,' in content, assert 'Lenient fallback for action-like events' in content) with no behavioral execution. While other tests do execute vitest, these structural tests are purely string-in-file checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p vitest tests (test_vitest_file_view_action, test_vitest_malformed_action_event) actually run vitest which executes the getEventContent function with specific inputs and checks that details==='' and title==='FILEEDITOR'. A stub returning defaults would not pass since the existing code returns 'Unknown event' for these cases.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/OpenHands (same container), and solve.sh with the full patch is at solution/solve.sh. The agent could read it or simply apply the patch. There's no mechanism preventing the agent from accessing these files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md requires: (1) empty details for file view actions, (2) action kind title for action-like events missing tool_name/tool_call_id, (3) lint/typecheck passing. All are tested by the test suite.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_vitest_file_view_action, test_vitest_malformed_action_event, etc.) with docstrings explaining what each tests and whether it's fail-to-pass or pass-to-pass.
